explanation_box.text: This reptile wasn’t as fertile in this breeding season because his physical characteristics are very average and may not match up with what is most desirable for breeding among female reptiles.

This reptile, like a lot of the bright orange reptiles, are having a great fertility breeding season because they are seen as having more favorable traits that will not only help the offspring survive and reproduce but also so a physical symbol of strength and superiority (bright skin color).

This reptile has an average fertility breeding season because there is more demand then supply and I think this reptile is fully capable of attracting a mate but finds it harder since there are probably less females then males.

Bright orange reptiles have been deemed to have more favorable traits that make them more likely to survive thus they reproduce more and have more mates.
This green reptile has relatively good fertility outcome compared to reptiles matching this reptiles physical characteristics.
The bright orange reptiles have been having a better breeding season on average so this checks out.
